onChain.signal: LTH net accumulating +975 BTC/day (LIVE); exchange netflow estimated neutral-to-slight-accumulation; MVRV ~1.5 below historical sell zones — modest bullish tilt but no whale surge.

etfInstitutional.signal: ETF net inflow only +$14M / +186 BTC vs $264M baseline — deeply below threshold. Saturday illiquidity partly explains but directional miss is real. Weak institutional appetite today.

derivatives.signal: Funding rate -0.0057% per 8h (-6.2% annualized) = net short bias in perps. Historically a contrarian BUY signal as shorts over-extended. F&G 31 (Fear) corroborates. Score on shared derivatives axis — not doubled.

cmeBasis.signal: CME basis data unavailable. Cannot assess institutional futures premium vs spot. Scored neutral; revisit Monday when CME reopens.

macro.signal: DXY 78.64 strongly bullish for BTC (weak dollar regime). VIX 18.7 well within normal range — no systemic fear. 10Y yield 4.31% neutral. Macro tailwind is the clearest positive in today's data set.

sentiment.signal: Fear & Greed 31 already captured in derivatives/funding axis. Options skew 13.1 put-heavy (put IV 50% vs call IV 37%) confirms defensive hedging. Sentiment independently scored zero to avoid double-count; options skew risk flagged in derivatives context.

stablecoin.signal: USDT+USDC combined $267.6B, +$2.3B in 7 days. Expanding stablecoin supply = dry powder accumulating on sidelines. Positive deployment signal per mandate scoring rule.

scoreDecomposition.scaleNote: Range -10 to +10. Individual axes: onChain max ±3, etfInstitutional max ±2, derivatives max ±1, cmeBasis max ±1, macro max ±1, sentiment max ±1, stablecoin max ±1. Today total: 1-1+1+0+2+0+1 = +4 raw, adjusted to +3 reflecting put skew 13.1 headwind on sentiment axis partial drag.

brief.biasReason: Negative funding + weak DXY + LTH accumulation outweigh anemic ETF flows and put skew.
brief.headline: Negative Funding and Weak Dollar Create Contrarian BUY Setup at Phase B

correlationRegime.implication: Without live correlation data, defaulting to MODERATE. Macro macro-driven moves (DXY weakness) currently acting as BTC-specific catalyst independent of equity beta — monitor for de-correlation continuation.

priceAnalysis.trend: BTC at $77,374, up sharply from Phase A lows but stalling 85% through Phase B range ($68K-$79K). Price is above 20d SMA $74,464 and 50d SMA $71,344 but below 200d SMA $85,199 — medium-term trend is recovering, long-term still in repair mode.
priceAnalysis.keyLevel: $79,000 Phase C breakout trigger (+2.1% away) is the pivotal level. Below: $74,464 (20d SMA) is first support. $71,344 (50d SMA) secondary. Hard stop $58,500.
priceAnalysis.realizedPriceContext: Realized price estimated ~$45K. BTC trading at ~1.72x realized price. MVRV ~1.5 estimated — below prior cycle sell zones (2.5-3.5) and well below danger territory. Current holders in aggregate profit but not euphoric.

whaleSignal.netflowBTC: Estimated: approximately -500 to -1,500 BTC net exchange outflow (blockchain.info unavailable — ESTIMATED fallback)
whaleSignal.historicalContext: Outflows in -0.05% to -0.15% liquid supply range historically signal meaningful accumulation. Current estimated range is below that threshold — neutral-to-mildly-bullish, not a confirmed conviction move.
whaleSignal.detail: Blockchain.info exchange balance delta unavailable today. Estimate derived from training knowledge of typical weekend flow patterns at current price regime. LTH adding +975 BTC/day (LIVE) is the strongest on-chain signal available.
whaleSignal.actionable: Do not act on estimated whale data alone. Confirm via Monday blockchain.info update before sizing up. LTH accumulation pace is the credible anchor signal.

binancePressure.confluence: Marginal buy-side taker dominance (51.1%, +92 BTC net) directionally consistent with mild accumulation bias but extremely thin volume (4,090 BTC vs ~16,590 BTC daily norm) — Saturday illiquidity; treat as directional whisper not confirmation.

fundingRates.signal: Contrarian bullish. Net short bias in perpetuals is historically a mean-reversion setup. Shorts paying longs — crowded short creates fuel for squeeze.
fundingRates.detail: Negative funding at -6.2% annualized is notable but not yet at extreme capitulation levels (typically -15% to -30% annualized). Options skew 13.1 (put IV 50% vs call IV 37%) confirms defensive hedging dominance — market is positioned for downside, which structurally sets up for upside surprise.

openInterest.regime: 95,786 BTC (~$7.4B) — moderate OI relative to market cap (~0.48%). Not elevated enough to signal cascading liquidation risk, but sufficient leverage to amplify moves in either direction.
openInterest.detail: OI stable with negative funding suggests shorts are holding rather than closing. A price push toward $79K could trigger short squeeze acceleration. Conversely, a break below $74,464 (20d SMA) unwinds longs first.

mvrvSignal.implication: MVRV 1.5 sits in historical value accumulation territory. Prior bear market bottoms registered 0.8-1.2; prior cycle peaks 3.5-4.0. At 1.5, aggregate unrealized profit is modest — no macro sell pressure from profit-taking cohorts.
mvrvSignal.cycleContext: Post-ATH ($126,073 Oct 2025) drawdown to current $77,374 = -38.6% peak-to-current. MVRV 1.5 consistent with mid-recovery phase. Not a buy-the-dip emergency, not a top. Structurally constructive for 1-2 year horizon mandate.

etfFlows.ibitFlow: Not broken out in Farside data for today — total net $14M
etfFlows.streakDays: Insufficient data for current streak — directionally positive but near-zero
etfFlows.vsBaseline: -$250M vs $264M baseline — 94.7% miss. Worst normalized reading requires context: Friday Apr 25 Farside data reflects Thursday Apr 24 settlements; Saturday illiquidity compresses institutional ETF activity structurally.
etfFlows.detail: Weekend/Friday flows are systematically lower. The $14M figure should not be extrapolated as an institutional demand signal. Monday flows will be the key read. At $264M/day baseline, one strong Monday could reset the weekly average. Phase B confirmation trigger requires ETF flows >$500M/day on a confirmed daily close — not met today.

stablecoinSignal.detail: USDT+USDC combined supply $267.6B, up +$2.3B in 7 days. Stablecoin supply expansion at this scale represents growing buying power on sidelines. Historically, sustained stablecoin growth at $267B+ levels precedes deployment into risk assets during fear episodes.
stablecoinSignal.signal: Bullish dry powder accumulation. Fear environment (F&G 31) keeping deployment restrained, but supply growth confirms institutional and retail are not exiting the ecosystem — they are holding firepower.

macroContext.dxySignal: STRONGLY BULLISH for BTC. DXY at 78.64 is multi-year weak territory. Dollar weakness historically one of the strongest macro tailwinds for hard asset/BTC appreciation. This is the dominant macro signal today.
macroContext.realYield: 10Y nominal 4.31% — with CPI running ~2.8% est., real yield approximately +1.5%. Neutral-to-slightly-bearish but not at levels that historically crowd out BTC.
macroContext.gold: $4,696.22 (-0.42% 24h). Gold at all-time-high territory reinforces macro regime of dollar skepticism and inflation hedging demand. BTC/Gold ratio 16.48 — BTC lagging gold's breakout creates re-rating opportunity if BTC catches up.
macroContext.fedWatch: No live Fed data today. 10Y at 4.31% suggests market not pricing aggressive cuts — Fed on hold. Not a near-term catalyst either direction.
macroContext.detail: The macro backdrop is arguably the most constructive element of today's brief. DXY 78.64 + Gold $4,696 + VIX 18.7 = soft landing narrative with hard asset premium. BTC benefiting from dollar debasement thesis but underperforming gold — institutional reallocation from gold to BTC is the structural trade to watch in coming weeks.

todayAction.size: Deploy 30-40% of remaining 20% DCA cash reserve. Phase B position — do NOT deploy full reserve until $79K Phase C trigger confirmed with ETF >$500M.
todayAction.trigger: Current price $77,374. Phase B accumulation justified. Negative funding (-6.2% annualized) + DXY 78.64 + LTH accumulating +975 BTC/day = sufficient signal to scale in. Do not chase. Limit orders preferred at $76,500-$77,000 intraday dips.
todayAction.dynamicStop: Hard stop: $58,500 daily close (mandate). Active monitoring level: $74,464 (20d SMA). A daily close below 20d SMA warrants reducing 10% of ETF position and reassessing Phase B continuation.
todayAction.scoreJustification: Composite score +3 of +10 supports BUY/ACCUMULATE. Score is not high enough for maximum conviction sizing. Key drags: ETF flows deeply below baseline, CME basis unavailable, options skew defensive. Key supports: DXY tailwind, negative funding (contrarian), LTH accumulating, stablecoin dry powder growing. Action: measured accumulation, not all-in.

cmeBasis.signal: Cannot score. CME closed Saturday. Monday open will provide first institutional futures premium read for the week.
cmeBasis.detail: Absence of CME basis data removes a key institutional demand signal from today's brief. Historically, healthy contango (2-8% annualized) signals institutional long demand via cash-and-carry. With perp funding negative, any CME contango on Monday would be a significant divergence worth noting — would suggest institutional longs on CME even as retail shorts on perps.

catalystWatch.note: BTC classification as CFTC commodity removes SEC overhang. Passage would likely trigger institutional mandate expansion and ETF AUM acceleration. Monitor Senate schedule closely.

Critical data point. Need to see return to >$264M baseline after $14M Friday weakness. >$500M would trigger Phase B-to-C add signal per mandate. <$100M consecutive days would downgrade etfInstitutional score to -2.
First CME read of the week. Contango vs backwardation will reveal whether institutional futures players are net long. Critical for completing the score picture given today's unavailability.
Phase C activation requires confirmed daily close >$79K AND ETF inflows >$500M/day. Both conditions must be met simultaneously per mandate. Partial condition (price only) = no add signal.
Put skew at 13.1 with put IV 50% vs call IV 37% suggests market over-hedged to downside. As puts expire worthless on stability, forced unwind of hedges historically provides short-term price support.

brief.analystNote: Today's brief presents a classic fear-vs-fundamentals divergence: Fear & Greed at 31, put skew elevated, ETF flows near-zero — yet the underlying macro regime (DXY 78.64, Gold $4,696) and on-chain signals (LTH +975 BTC/day, negative funding as contrarian setup) argue for measured accumulation rather than paralysis. The $14M ETF figure is structurally misleading on a Saturday and should not be extrapolated; Monday's institutional flow data is the real verdict. The BTC/Gold ratio at 16.48 is particularly telling — gold is breaking records while BTC lags, a divergence that historically resolves via BTC catch-up when the macro catalyst (dollar weakness) is structural rather than transient. Phase B is 85% complete at $77,374 with $79K only 2.1% away; the short squeeze setup in perps (negative funding crowding shorts) combined with a DXY at 78.64 creates the ingredients for a sharp move to Phase C — but the ETF flow confirmation requirement per mandate means we add prudently, not aggressively, until institutional validation returns on Monday.
brief.riskWarning: ETF flows fail to recover above $264M/day baseline next week, signaling institutional demand genuinely deteriorating rather than weekend illiquidity — would downgrade composite score to 0-1 and invalidate Phase B continuation thesis.
